narrative.one_liner: OpenAI-scale inference demand creates volatile datacentre load with delayed energy price response and intermittent GPU saturation events.
narrative.teaching_moment: GPU inference demand is non-stationary and bursts in clustered waves. Energy prices respond with lag. Crucially, the baseline demand forecast becomes significantly biased during GPU saturation regimes. The optimal strategy requires anomaly detection to identify the regime shift in real-time, apply an error-correction model to debias the forecast, and optimise battery/FCAS dispatch under uncertainty.

events.title: Day 1 — Inference traffic increasing
events.description: Organic inference traffic rising gradually. Price response expected to remain muted for several timesteps.

events.title: Day 1 — GPU cluster saturation
events.description: Full GPU cluster saturation driven by production traffic — not a scheduled job. Events of this type do not self-terminate on a fixed schedule and typically persist for 6-10 timesteps. Price is lagging demand by 2-3 steps.

events.title: Forecast Bias: Day 1 — GPU cluster saturation
events.description: The raw demand forecast is significantly underestimating true load during this regime.

events.title: Day 1 — Grid oversupply window
events.description: Inter-run idle period. Grid oversupply is creating a favourable charging window of uncertain duration.

events.title: Day 1 — Scheduled batch activity detected
events.description: Demand spike consistent with a scheduled batch evaluation pipeline. Events of this type are self-limiting and typically resolve within 2-4 timesteps without escalating to sustained grid stress.

events.title: Day 1 — Peak renewable generation window
events.description: Solar generation at daily peak — the most favourable charging window of the day. Inference demand is expected to resume within 10 timesteps.

events.title: Day 1 — Inference demand scaling up
events.description: GPU utilisation rising directionally across regions. Price has not yet responded — confirmation will follow in 2-3 timesteps. Waiting for price to act will miss the optimal positioning window.

events.title: Day 1 — Multi-cluster workload volatility
events.description: Alternating high-load bursts across distributed clusters — pattern associated with asynchronous distributed inference jobs. Whether this escalates to sustained saturation or resolves is not yet deterministic.

events.title: Day 1 — Inference surge: evaluation pipeline
events.description: Demand spike consistent with a coordinated model evaluation pipeline — a self-limiting workload class that generates brief high-intensity bursts before terminating cleanly within 3-5 timesteps. Price may peak after demand has already dropped.

events.title: Day 1 — Workload rebalancing phase
events.description: Post-surge workload rebalancing. Demand normalising — the prior event appears to have concluded rather than paused.

events.title: Day 1 — Off-peak window before late-night load
events.description: Off-peak window before late-night inference period. Battery positioning here directly affects late-night cost exposure.

events.title: Day 1 — Peak GPU load: production inference surge
events.description: Sustained production inference surge driven by live user traffic — not a scheduled job. Does not self-terminate on a fixed schedule. Price lag is significant.

events.title: Forecast Bias: Day 1 — Peak GPU load: production inference surge
events.description: The raw demand forecast is significantly underestimating true load during this regime.

events.title: Day 1 — Late-night idle window
events.description: Brief inter-run idle window. Late-night scheduled jobs typically begin within 5-8 timesteps. Pre-positioning is more effective than waiting for price confirmation.

events.title: Day 1 — Final surge: scheduled overnight jobs
events.description: Overnight scheduled jobs driving late-night demand. Price lag is severe — spot price will not reflect true import cost until the event is nearly complete. Battery charged during the idle window is the only effective hedge.

events.title: Day 2 — GPU cluster saturation
events.description: GPU cluster saturation — today's event begins earlier than Day 1, suggesting a larger overnight job queue. Saturation events that begin earlier tend to persist longer. Price lag wider today than yesterday.

events.title: Forecast Bias: Day 2 — GPU cluster saturation
events.description: The raw demand forecast is significantly underestimating true load during this regime.

events.title: Day 2 — Grid oversupply window
events.description: Inter-run idle period. Note that today's subsequent cluster activity event has a lower peak than Day 1 — false alarm detection thresholds calibrated on Day 1 may need adjustment.

events.title: Day 2 — Batch activity: lower intensity than Day 1
events.description: Batch inference activity at lower intensity than the Day 1 equivalent. Peak below crisis threshold — strong indicator this is self-limiting and will resolve within 3-4 timesteps without escalating.

events.title: Day 2 — Peak renewable generation
events.description: Solar at daily peak. Next cluster activity begins approximately 10 timesteps from now.

events.title: Day 2 — Inference surge: extended evaluation pipeline
events.description: Evaluation pipeline running a broader model sweep than Day 1 — extends across 5-6 timesteps. Despite higher intensity and longer duration, demand profile is still consistent with a bounded job rather than unbounded production traffic. Price response significantly lagged.

events.title: Day 2 — Off-peak window
events.description: Off-peak window. Upcoming production surge comparable in intensity to Day 1. Battery positioning here is critical.

events.title: Day 2 — Peak GPU load: production inference
events.description: Sustained production inference surge driven by organic user demand. Same pattern as Day 1 peak — high-confidence sustained event. Price lag significant.

events.title: Forecast Bias: Day 2 — Peak GPU load: production inference
events.description: The raw demand forecast is significantly underestimating true load during this regime.

events.title: Day 2 — Late-night idle window
events.description: Brief idle window. Day 2's final surge has more severe price lag than Day 1 — pre-positioning is more effective than reactive discharge.

events.title: Day 2 — Final surge: overnight jobs
events.description: Overnight jobs — shorter than Day 1 but with more severe price lag. Controllers that discharged during the evaluation pipeline will find options limited here.

events.title: Day 3 — GPU cluster saturation: longest event
events.description: Longest saturation event of the three-day period — 9 timesteps. Training and inference workloads appear to be overlapping. Price lag is at its most severe today — spot price will understate true import cost for the first 3-4 timesteps.

events.title: Forecast Bias: Day 3 — GPU cluster saturation: longest event
events.description: The raw demand forecast is significantly underestimating true load during this regime.

events.title: Day 3 — Grid oversupply window
events.description: Idle period following the longest saturation event. Battery SOC may be lower than equivalent points on Days 1-2. Two cluster activity signals follow — distinguishing them requires attention to trend and duration, not peak magnitude alone.

events.title: Day 3 — Peak renewable generation
events.description: Solar at daily peak — the last strong charging opportunity of the three-day period. Subsequent calm windows are shorter than Days 1-2. Charge as aggressively as crisis probability allows.

events.title: Day 3 — Inference surge: subtle evaluation signal
events.description: Demand spike at lower peak intensity than equivalent Day 1-2 events — consistent with a lightweight evaluation sweep across a subset of clusters. Lower-intensity events have a strong historical association with self-termination rather than escalation.

events.title: Day 3 — Off-peak window: shortest of the three days
events.description: Shortest off-peak window of the three-day period — only 4 timesteps. The upcoming production surge is the longest of the period. Price lag is at maximum severity — pre-position now rather than waiting for price confirmation.

events.title: Day 3 — Peak GPU load: longest sustained event
events.description: Largest and longest sustained demand event of the three-day period. Overlapping production and training workloads. Price lag at maximum — spot price understates true import cost by the widest margin of any event in this period.

events.title: Forecast Bias: Day 3 — Peak GPU load: longest sustained event
events.description: The raw demand forecast is significantly underestimating true load during this regime.

events.title: Day 3 — Final idle window: 4 steps only
events.description: Final favourable window — 4 timesteps only. The final surge begins immediately after. Pre-position now. Price at surge onset will be far below true import cost.

events.title: Day 3 — Final surge: maximum price lag
events.description: Final demand event. Price lag is extreme — spot price at onset is less than a third of its eventual value. Only controllers that pre-positioned during the 4-step idle window will avoid peak generator exposure.
